---
# generated by https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-docs
page_title: "parse_bgp_rd_rt function - terraform-provider-utils"
subcategory: ""
description: |-
  Parse a BGP Route Distinguisher or Route Target from colon notation
---

# function: parse_bgp_rd_rt

Takes a BGP RD/RT in standard colon notation (e.g., '65000:1001', '192.168.100.1:1002', '4200000001:1003') and returns an object with the detected format type and parsed components. Supports three format types per RFC 4364/4360: 'two_byte_as' (AS <= 65535), 'four_byte_as' (AS > 65535), and 'ipv4_address' (IPv4:value).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Parse a Two-byte AS Route Target (AS <= 65535)
output "rt_two_byte" {
  value = provider::utils::parse_bgp_rd_rt("65000:1001")
  # Output: { format = "two_byte_as", as_number = 65000, assigned_number = 1001, ipv4_address = "" }
}

# Parse a Four-byte AS Route Distinguisher (AS > 65535)
output "rd_four_byte" {
  value = provider::utils::parse_bgp_rd_rt("4200000001:1003")
  # Output: { format = "four_byte_as", as_number = 4200000001, assigned_number = 1003, ipv4_address = "" }
}

# Parse an IPv4 Address Route Target
output "rd_ipv4" {
  value = provider::utils::parse_bgp_rd_rt("192.168.100.1:1002")
  # Output: { format = "ipv4_address", as_number = 0, assigned_number = 1002, ipv4_address = "192.168.100.1" }
}

# Use in resource configuration
variable "user_rd" {
  type    = string
  default = "65001:100"
}

locals {
  bgp_rd = try(provider::utils::parse_bgp_rd_rt(var.user_rd), null)
}

resource "example_bgp_rd" "bgp_rd" {
  bgp_rd_two_byte_as_number  = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "two_byte_as" ? local.bgp_rd.as_number : null, null)
  bgp_rd_two_byte_as_index   = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "two_byte_as" ? local.bgp_rd.assigned_number : null, null)
  bgp_rd_four_byte_as_number = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "four_byte_as" ? local.bgp_rd.as_number : null, null)
  bgp_rd_four_byte_as_index  = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "four_byte_as" ? local.bgp_rd.assigned_number : null, null)
  bgp_rd_ipv4_address        = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "ipv4_address" ? local.bgp_rd.ipv4_address : null, null)
  bgp_rd_ipv4_address_index  = try(local.bgp_rd.format == "ipv4_address" ? local.bgp_rd.assigned_number : null, null)
}
```

## Return Object

The function returns an object with the following attributes:

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `format` | String | Detected format: `"two_byte_as"`, `"four_byte_as"`, or `"ipv4_address"` |
| `as_number` | Number | Administrator subfield as AS number (populated for `two_byte_as` and `four_byte_as`; `0` for `ipv4_address`) |
| `assigned_number` | Number | Assigned Number subfield (always populated) |
| `ipv4_address` | String | Administrator subfield as IPv4 address (populated for `ipv4_address`; `""` for AS formats) |

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
parse_bgp_rd_rt(value string) object
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `value` (String) BGP RD/RT in colon notation (e.g., '65000:1001', '192.168.100.1:1002', '4200000001:1003').
